tiny_practice.action: Take a deep breath and silently ask God to help you reflect on inner beauty and gentle strength

two_step.reflect_question: How can I demonstrate Christ-like character in my most challenging relationships?

options.text: Prioritize spiritual character over external appearance
options.response: Spend 10 minutes in prayer instead of extra grooming

options.text: Seek to win others through gentle conduct
options.response: Choose compassion over being right in a conflict

creative_prompt.instruction: Draw a heart with different symbols representing inner beauty: kindness, gentleness, humility

reevaluation.question: How has this chapter changed my understanding of personal worth?
options: I now value inner character more than external appearance
I recognize the power of gentle, respectful relationships
I understand true beauty comes from spiritual strength
I feel challenged to grow in emotional and spiritual maturity

three_choice.question: Which aspect of spiritual transformation resonates most deeply?

choices.expansion: True transformation begins in the heart, not through cosmetic changes
Gentleness can be more powerful than confrontation in spiritual influence
Trials can be redemptive pathways for spiritual growth and witness

socratic_nudge.question: How might your deepest vulnerabilities actually be portals for divine grace and transformation?

moral_slider.question: Between personal autonomy and spiritual submission, where do you locate authentic freedom?

reevaluation.question: How has your understanding of spiritual influence shifted?
options: Inner character matters more than external performance

thematic_summaries.title: Christian Marriage Dynamics
thematic_summaries.summary: Peter addresses the transformative power of godly conduct within marital relationships. He emphasizes spiritual influence through character over external demands or manipulation.

thematic_summaries.title: Inner Beauty and Spiritual Transformation
thematic_summaries.summary: The passage highlights the supreme value of internal spiritual qualities over external appearances. Genuine spiritual beauty emerges from a gentle, humble, and Christ-centered heart.

reevaluation.question: How should Christian marriages reflect divine love and redemptive transformation?
options: Through mutual submission and spiritual accountability
By maintaining traditional gender role hierarchies
Prioritizing spiritual character over cultural expectations
Emphasizing individual spiritual growth within partnership
